Atrium now tells you what changed. After it updates itself, it shows you the release notes for the version you just landed on — and these are the first notes it'll surface.

## Highlights

- **"What's new" after an update.** When Atrium auto-updates to a newer version, the next launch shows a one-time overlay with that version's release notes, then gets out of your way. It appears only after a real version bump — never on a fresh install, and never twice for the same version. If a fetch fails (offline right after updating), nothing is recorded, so you'll still see the notes once you're back online.
- **On by default, easy to silence.** The overlay is controlled by `show_release_notes_after_update` (default on). Toggle it under *Behavior* in the in-app settings panel, or set it in `config.json`.
- **Hand-written notes, not a commit dump.** Releases can now ship curated notes (this file is the first) instead of an auto-generated changelog — so what you read on update leads with what changed for *you*.

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ZviBaratz/atrium/main/install.sh | bash
```

Or with Go — note this builds from source and reports a `dev` version (the
script installs the version-stamped, self-updating release binary):

```bash
go install github.com/ZviBaratz/atrium@v0.6.0
```

This is a 0.x release — APIs and behavior may change.
